steps.rationale: If the task is to turn noisy transcript output into a polished outpatient note, model capacity is likely part of the bottleneck. A stronger note-generation model paired with a more explicit clinical style should improve fidelity, plan specificity, and presentation.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact fails as an evaluation note despite passing the contract mechanically. It omits nearly all clinically relevant content from the case and expected note, replaces it with irrelevant meta commentary, and provides no usable structure, assessment detail, plan, or return precautions. Overall, this is far below the expected standard for a clinical follow-up note.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Upgrade note generation model and prompt for fuller clinical synthesis and cleaner plan/precautions.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Upgrade note generation model and prompt for fuller clinical synthesis and cleaner plan/precautions.`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> model changed from `gpt-5-mini` to `gpt-5`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `structured_clinical`

steps.rationale: Several misses look upstream as well: aggressive trimming and strict VAD can drop low-volume details like negatives, meds, allergy, and precautions. Relaxing preprocessing while keeping the same generator should improve factual capture before note synthesis.
steps.judge_justification: Overall this artifact performs very poorly against the rubric. It captures only the broad complaint, omits almost all clinically necessary details, provides no usable assessment/plan/precautions, and contains clear nonclinical hallucinated leakage about pipeline settings rather than patient care. Despite passing the contract, it is not a satisfactory clinical follow-up note.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Higher-recall audio preprocessing to preserve low-volume clinical details for downstream note generation.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Higher-recall audio preprocessing to preserve low-volume clinical details for downstream note generation.`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> denoise changed from `off` to `on`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> silence trimming changed from `aggressive` to `moderate`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> vad changed from `strict` to `balanced`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> voice enhancement changed from `off` to `on`

steps.rationale: The main failure is note-generation style: the output is too brief, leaks pipeline commentary, and omits standard clinical sections. Tightening the note prompt toward a chart-ready SOAP/HPI-style note should improve completeness and tone without changing the rest of the pipeline.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact performs very poorly against the rubric. It captures only the patient identity and general complaint, while omitting nearly every clinically important detail from the case and expected note. It also contains obvious nonclinical leakage about pipeline/demo settings, which undermines factual fidelity and professional style. Overall, this is not an adequate clinical follow-up note and is far from the expected level of completeness, structure, and chart-readiness.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Chart-ready clinical note prompt with explicit sectioning and no transcript/pipeline meta language.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Chart-ready clinical note prompt with explicit sectioning and no transcript/pipeline meta language.`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `structured_clinical`
